name: Nose bleed
summary: Common in children. Rarely serious. Usually stops with simple pressure.
recognition: Bleeding from one or both nostrils

callEmergency.text: Call 999 if bleeding hasn't stopped after 30 minutes, OR the casualty is on anti-coagulants (e.g. warfarin).

steps: Sit them down. Tip head FORWARD (not back).
Pinch the soft part of the nose just above the nostrils.
Hold for 10 constant minutes — no peeking.

warnings.text: Don't tip the head back — blood runs down the throat, can cause choking or vomiting.
